symptoms: Persistent worry about various aspects of daily life
Physical symptoms: racing heart, sweating, trembling
Panic attacks or sudden intense fear
causes: Chronic stress from work or relationships
Medical conditions or medication side effects
coping_techniques: Deep breathing exercises: Try 4-7-8 breathing or box breathing
Grounding techniques: 5-4-3-2-1 sensory exercise to bring focus to present
Progressive muscle relaxation: Tense and release muscle groups
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT): Challenge and reframe anxious thoughts
Physical activity: Exercise releases endorphins and reduces stress
Limit caffeine and alcohol: These can trigger or worsen anxiety
Maintain regular sleep schedule: Sleep deprivation amplifies anxiety
Social support: Talk with trusted friends or family
Professional help: Therapy or medication when anxiety becomes overwhelming
subtopics.when_to_seek_help: If anxiety persists for more than 2 weeks, interferes with daily life, or causes physical symptoms

symptoms: Persistent sad or empty mood lasting weeks or months
Loss of interest in activities you once enjoyed
self_care_strategies: Establish a routine: Structure helps create stability and purpose
Move your body: Even 10-15 min walks can boost mood
Exposure to sunlight: Vitamin D and natural light help regulate mood
Maintain basic hygiene: Shower, change clothes, basic self-care
Reach out to others: Combat isolation by connecting with friends
Engage in enjoyable activities: Even small things like favorite music
Avoid alcohol and drugs: These worsen depression
Practice self-compassion: Treat yourself as you'd treat a friend
Keep a journal: Reflect on feelings and patterns
Seek professional support: Therapy and medication are effective
subtopics.when_to_seek_help: If you experience persistent sadness for more than 2 weeks, thoughts of suicide, or inability to function

work_life_balance: Set clear boundaries: Don't check work emails after hours
Schedule breaks during the day: Even 5 minutes helps reset
Take vacations and use time off: Rest is essential for mental health
Delegate tasks: You don't have to do everything yourself
Say no to non-essential commitments: Protect your time and energy
Create a separate workspace if working from home
Establish transitions: Separate work time from personal time
Pursue hobbies and interests outside work
mindfulness: Daily meditation: Start with just 5-10 minutes
Mindful breathing: Focus on each inhale and exhale
Body scan meditation: Notice sensations without judgment
Mindful eating: Eat slowly and notice flavors and textures
Walking meditation: Pay attention to each step
Mindful listening: Fully engage when others speak
Present-moment awareness: Notice what's happening right now
time_management: Identify your priorities: Focus on what truly matters
Break large tasks into smaller steps: Reduces overwhelm
Use time-blocking: Dedicate blocks for specific activities
Minimize distractions: Create a focused work environment
Take regular breaks: Rest improves productivity and mental health
Learn to say no: Protect your time from endless demands
Track time usage: Understand where your time actually goes

digital_wellness: Curate your feed: Follow positive, inspiring, and authentic accounts
Set time limits: Use app timers to limit social media use
Remove notifications: Reduce constant alerts and distractions
No phones before bed: Protect sleep quality
Compare less: Remember that curated posts aren't reality
Unfollow accounts that make you feel bad: Your mental health matters
Take digital detox days: Regular breaks from screens
comparison_culture: Understand the curated nature: People show highlight reels, not real life
Remember filters and editing: Photos are heavily manipulated
Focus on your own journey: Progress is personal and non-linear
Practice gratitude: Appreciate what you have instead of comparing
Limit comparison triggers: Mute or unfollow unhelpful accounts
Reality check: Ask 'Is this realistic?' when viewing content
Celebrate others' wins: Reduce envy by practicing generosity
healthy_boundaries: Know your limits: When to step back from social media
Be authentic: Share your real self, not a perfect version
Mute conflict: You don't need to engage in every argument
Protect your privacy: Share only what you're comfortable with
Manage notifications: Turn off alerts from draining interactions
Set usage rules: When and where you use social media

recognition: Persistent self-doubt despite evidence of competence
Attributing success to luck or timing, not your abilities
Fear of being 'found out' as a fraud
Over-preparing or working longer hours to mask insecurity
Comparing yourself to others and feeling inferior
Perfectionism and fear of making mistakes
reframing_techniques: Document your achievements: Keep a 'success file' of accomplishments
Accept compliments gracefully: Say 'thank you' instead of deflecting
Challenge the 'fraud' thought: Ask for evidence it's actually true
Recognize learning requires time: Everyone was a beginner once
Share your struggles: Others feel the same way
Focus on effort and growth: Not just innate talent
Remember failures are data: They teach you what to do differently
Celebrate small wins: Progress is progress, no matter how small
coping_strategies: Seek mentorship: Learn from others who've overcome similar feelings
Join communities: Connect with people in your field
Take on challenges: Small wins build confidence
Practice self-compassion: You're allowed to be imperfect
Reframe anxiety as excitement: Same physical sensation, different meaning
Focus on helping others: Builds confidence and provides perspective

connection_strategies: Reach out first: Send a message, make a call, invite someone
Join groups or clubs: Find people with shared interests
Volunteer: Help others while building meaningful connections
Attend social events: Even if you feel anxious, start small
Use technology: Video calls, online communities, social media
Be vulnerable: Share genuine feelings to deepen connections
Practice active listening: Make others feel heard and valued
Schedule regular meetups: Consistency strengthens friendships
community_building: Find your tribe: Look for people who share your values
Online communities: Reddit, Discord, forums for any interest
Classes or workshops: Learn something while connecting
Support groups: Specialized groups for specific challenges
Faith communities: If spiritual, churches and spiritual centers
Work relationships: Develop friendships beyond just work tasks
Neighborhood activities: Community events, block parties, etc.
self_connection: Develop a relationship with yourself: Self-compassion and self-care
Hobbies and interests: Creative and fulfilling activities
Journaling: Explore your thoughts and feelings
Meditation and mindfulness: Connect with your inner self
Physical activity: Movement is a form of self-connection
Therapy: A therapeutic relationship can be deeply connecting

emergency_hotlines: Crisis Text Line: Text HOME to 741741
SAMHSA National Helpline: 1-800-662-HELP (4357) - Substance abuse and mental health
Immigration-specific support: Available through community organizations
support_organizations: National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI): www.nami.org
International Association for Suicide Prevention: https://www.iasp.info/
Local emergency services: 911 (US) for immediate danger
immediate_actions: Contact emergency services (911) if in immediate danger
Call or text a crisis helpline: They are trained to help
Tell someone you trust: A friend, family member, or professional
Go to the nearest emergency room if needed
Remove means of self-harm if possible
Create a safety plan with the help of a mental health professional
Schedule an appointment with a mental health provider urgently
